mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and a decline in housing starts signals supply-side bottlenecks outpacing demand, triggering input cost inflation that compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly accelerating consumer price inflation combined with rising policy rates forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive monetary conditions, elevating real discount rates and suppressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional correlation between the inflation-rate spread and SPX returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real personal savings balances indicate elevated household liquidity buffers, which translate into higher discretionary consumption and corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 12-month rolling window, or forward returns in high-savings regimes are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth below 1.5 percent annualized signals an approaching economic slowdown, reducing earnings revision momentum and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances following the GDP growth threshold breach, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.7 million units annually reflect robust builder confidence and mortgage demand, which historically lead equity market rallies by stimulating construction-related corporate earnings and wealth effects over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in over 60% of triggered episodes, or the signal's rank correlation with SPX returns remains below 0.04 across all rolling 2-year periods.
